name: Iraqi Armed Forces (ISF)
detail: Iraqi Security Forces comprise ~200,000 active personnel across Army, Air Force, Navy, Counter-Terrorism Service (CTS), and Federal Police. Rebuilt after 2014 collapse with US Coalition training and equipment. CTS (known as the 'Golden Division') is considered the most capable unit and spearheaded the Mosul offensive. Chronic challenges: ghost soldiers on payroll, politicized officer corps, poor logistics, and command divided between Baghdad and PMF.

name: Kurdish Peshmerga
detail: The Kurdistan Region's ~200,000 Peshmerga fighters are divided between KDP-aligned forces (Zerevani) and PUK-aligned forces — a legacy of the 1990s Kurdish civil war. Peshmerga played a critical role in containing ISIS after 2014 and participated in the Mosul offensive. Poorly equipped compared to US/NATO standards but battle-hardened. Budget funding from Baghdad remains disputed; US coalition training continues.

name: Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF/Hashd al-Shaabi)
detail: Formed after Grand Ayatollah Sistani's 2014 fatwa, the PMF/Hashd al-Shaabi comprises 67+ factions with ~150,000 fighters. Formally integrated into Iraqi Security Forces under the 2016 PMF Law but many factions maintain parallel command chains answering to Iran's IRGC Quds Force. Key factions: Kata'ib Hezbollah (Iran-armed, designated FTO by US), Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq, Badr Organization, Kata'ib Sayyid al-Shuhada. Non-Iran-aligned factions include Saraya al-Salam (Sadrists) and Kata'ib al-Imam Ali.

name: US-Led Coalition (Operation Inherent Resolve)
detail: US-led Combined Joint Task Force – Operation Inherent Resolve (CJTF-OIR) formed in 2014 against ISIS. At peak: ~5,200 US troops plus 2,000+ coalition partners. Conducted 34,000+ airstrikes in Iraq and Syria (2014-2019). Transitioning to advisory role under 2024 US-Iraq security agreement; ~2,500 US troops remaining as of 2024. Key missions: ISF/Peshmerga training, intelligence sharing, logistics, and special operations raids against ISIS leadership.

name: Iranian IRGC Quds Force
detail: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Quds Force provides arms, training, financing, and command guidance to multiple Iran-aligned PMF factions in Iraq. Qasem Soleimani directed Iranian strategy in Iraq until his killing in January 2020; replaced by Esmail Qaani. The IRGC-Quds Force oversees a network enabling Iran to project power across the 'Resistance Axis' (Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, Yemen). Operations in Iraq include missile/drone attacks on US bases and weapons transfers transiting Iraq to Syria and Lebanon.

name: ISIS (Islamic State) — Insurgent Phase
detail: After territorial defeat (December 2017), ISIS transitioned to a decentralized insurgency with ~10,000–20,000 fighters estimated in Iraq and Syria (US Defense Intelligence Agency 2021). Operating primarily in the Hamrin Mountains, Tigris River valley, and desert areas of Anbar, Diyala, Kirkuk, and Salah al-Din provinces. Tactics: IED ambushes, assassinations of local officials, extortion of farming communities, and occasional complex attacks. ~500 attacks in Iraq in 2024.
